title: Indirect Evaporative Cooling Applicability
summary: Applicable in climates with low wet-bulb temperatures where significant cooling is available; can also be used in humid climates during non-peak seasons.

structured_payload.application_condition: low wet-bulb climates and non-peak seasons in humid climates

evidence_quote: This may be used in climates with low wet-bulb temperatures where significant amounts of cooling are available. In such climates, the size of the conventional cooling system can also be reduced.

title: Indirect Evaporative Cooling Air-Side Pressure Drop
summary: Typical air-side pressure drop increase due to an additional coil in the airstream.

structured_payload.value_range: 0.2 to 0.4 in. of water [50 to 100 Pa]

evidence_quote: Air-side pressure drop (typically 0.2 to 0.4 in. of water [50 to 100 Pa]) increases due to an additional coil in the airstream.

title: Thermally Actuated Diffusers Application
summary: Can be used in any all-air systems and are generally economically feasible when compared to a VAV box serving less than five air outlets.

structured_payload.application_condition: all-air systems, economically feasible when VAV box serves <5 air outlets

evidence_quote: Thermally actuated diffusers can be used in any all-air systems and are generally found economically feasible when compared to a VAV box is serving less than five air outlets.

title: Thermally Actuated Diffusers Connection to BMS
summary: Basic models cannot connect to the Building Management System (BMS).
evidence_quote: Basic models cannot connect to the BMS

title: Automated Refrigerant Leak Detection Application
summary: Typically used in mechanical rooms where chillers are installed.
evidence_quote: Automated refrigerant leakage detection and recovery systems are typically used in mechanical rooms where chillers are installed.

title: Chiller Annual Refrigerant Loss Rate
summary: Some chillers lose 2% to 15% of their refrigerant charge annually through different mechanisms.
evidence_quote: Some chillers lose 2% to 15% of their refrigerant charge annually through different mechanisms.

title: Refrigerant Sensor Installation Height
summary: Refrigerant sensor is generally installed 8 in. (200 mm) above the ground near both ends of every chiller.
evidence_quote: the refrigerant sensor (based on the particular refrigerant used in the chillers) is generally installed 8 in. (200 mm) above the ground near both ends of every chiller.

title: Refrigerant Recovery Nominal Pressure
summary: System recovers refrigerant until a nominal pressure of around 3 to 4 psig (25 kPa) is achieved.
evidence_quote: The system will recover the refrigerant until a nominal pressure of around 3 to 4 psig (25 kPa) is achieved.

title: R134a Liquid Refrigerant Temperature During Recovery
summary: For R134a, liquid refrigerant temperature during recovery at 25 kPa is likely around -5°F to -10°F (roughly -22°C).
evidence_quote: for R134a it is likely to be around -5°F to -10°F (roughly -22°C).

title: Desiccant Replacement Interval
summary: Desiccant usually must be replaced, replenished, or reconditioned every 5 to 10 years.
evidence_quote: Desiccant usually must be replaced, replenished, or reconditioned every 5 to 10 years.
